title: Jamaican Pecan Chicken Stew
description: A flavorful stew that combines the spices of Jamaica with the crunch of pecans. This dish is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Season the chicken breast with Jamaican jerk seasoning and cook until browned on both sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and chopped bell pepper to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the stew mix, monsoon spice blend, and chopped pecans to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Return the chicken to the pot and reduce heat to low.
Simmer the stew for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the sour cream and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
